analysis.summary: The technician reconfigured the network adapter of a virtual machine (TE-DC-BK-VBR-01) within the vSphere Client. The adapter was changed from DPG-254-Technijian to DPG-iSCSI-B. This process involved powering off and then powering on the VM to apply the changes.
actions_taken: Closed a remote console connection warning in Server Manager.
Observed services listed in Server Manager.
Navigated to the 'TE-DC-BK-VBR-01' VM within the vSphere Client.
Sent Ctrl+Alt+Delete to the VM console.
issues_observed: Initial Server Manager warning about existing remote console connections.
VM's Network adapter 1 was connected to 'DPG-254-Technijian' instead of the intended 'DPG-iSCSI-B'.
issues_resolved: The network adapter setting for 'TE-DC-BK-VBR-01' was successfully reconfigured to 'DPG-iSCSI-B'.
notable_events: The VM required a power cycle (off and then on) to apply the network adapter configuration change.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician worked efficiently and methodically, directly navigating to the target VM and executing the required network configuration change, including the necessary power cycle.
